channel.title: Microsoft Learn - in Intune
channel.description: The latest updates from Microsoft Learn in Intune

item.title: Microsoft Cloud PKI for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: An overview of the Microsoft Cloud PKI service, available with Microsoft Intune Suite or as an Intune add-on.

item.title: Use SCEP certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create and assign Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol (SCEP) certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Wipe - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to wipe, or factory reset, devices with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure infrastructure to support SCEP certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: To use Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol (SCEP) with Microsoft Intune, configure your on-premises AD domain, create a certification authority, and set up the NDES server to support use of the Certificate Connector.

item.title: Assign Microsoft Intune licenses - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Assign licenses to users so they can enroll in Intune

item.title: Configure Endpoint Analytics - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step-by-step guide to configure endpoint analytics in Microsoft Intune, enroll devices, and manage data collection policies for device insights.

item.title: Configure Microsoft Cloud PKI root and issuing CA for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure a root and issuing CA for the Microsoft Cloud PKI service.

item.title: Use a PKCS certificate profile to provision devices with certificates in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Public Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) certificates with Microsoft Intune, work with root certificates and certificate templates, and use device configuration profiles for a PKCS Certificate.

item.title: Bring your own certificate authority with Cloud PKI - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create a Microsoft Cloud PKI BYOCA issuing CA in Intune and anchor it to an on-premises or private CA.

item.title: Device Action: Retire - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to retire device using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Locate Device - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Locate lost or stolen devices by using the locate device feature in Microsoft Intune. Get details on security and privacy information when using the locate device action.

item.title: Create trusted certificate profiles in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create and deploy trusted certificate profiles to deploy a trusted root certificate to managed devices in Intune. Trusted certificate profiles support use of Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol (SCEP) and Public Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Collect Diagnostics - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to collect diagnostics with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Microsoft Intune cloud PKI fundamentals - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Describes fundamentals for Microsoft Intune cloud PKI.

item.title: Types of certificate that are supported by Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Microsoft Intune's support for Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol (SCEP), Public Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) certificates.

item.title: Set the mobile device management authority - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to set the mobile device management authority in Microsoft Intune and some key considerations.

item.title: Device Action: Delete - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to delete devices with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Reports for Windows Feature Update Policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the reports available for Windows feature update policies in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Find the primary user of a Microsoft Intune device. - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find the primary user (or User Device Affinity) of an Intune device.

item.title: Automatically Hide Devices With Cleanup Rules - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Intune's device cleanup rules offer a simple, automated way to ensure that only actively managed devices remain visible in the admin center. Learn more about device cleanup rules and how to configure them.

item.title: Use imported PFX certificates in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use imported Public Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) certificates with Microsoft Intune. Import certificates, configure certificate templates, and create an Imported PKCS Certificate profile.

item.title: Expedite Policies for Windows Quality Updates - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use expedite policies in Microsoft Intune to quickly deploy a specific Windows security update, bypass deferrals, and monitor installation status across devices.

item.title: Add groups to organize users and devices for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create Microsoft Entra groups to organize users and devices for use with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Zebra LifeGuard Over-the-Air Integration with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to manage firmware updates for supported Zebra devices.

item.title: Use Hotpatch With Windows Quality Updates - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how hotpatch works with Windows quality update policies in Microsoft Intune to install eligible security updates without requiring an immediate device restart.

item.title: Use derived credentials for mobile devices with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use a third-party partners derived credential as an authentication method for Intune.

item.title: Use third-party certification authorities (CA) with SCEP in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: In Microsoft Intune, you can add a vendor or third-party certificate authority (CA) to issue certificates to mobile devices using the SCEP protocol. In this overview, a Microsoft Entra application gives Microsoft Intune permissions to validate certificates. Then, use the application ID, authentication key, and tenant ID of the Microsoft Entra application in the setup of your SCEP server to issue certificates.

item.title: Android FOTA Updates - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to manage firmware updates on Android devices. A FOTA update can include software and security patches, feature updates, and other changes to the device's firmware.

item.title: Deployment models for Microsoft Intune cloud PKI - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Describes the deployment options for Microsoft Intune cloud PKI.

item.title: Configure Rollout Options for Feature Update Policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure rollout options in feature update policies to control when Windows feature updates become available to devices and deploy updates gradually.

item.title: Device Action: Update Cellular Data Plan - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to update the cellular data plan for iOS/iPadOS devices that support eSIM with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Reports for Windows Driver Update Policies reports - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use Intune reports to monitor Windows driver updates for devices assigned to Windows Driver update policies.

item.title: Use the Device Offboarding Agent - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the Device Offboarding Agent in Microsoft Intune to identify and offboard devices that are no longer in use.

item.title: Issue DigiCert PKCS certificates with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Install and configure the Certificate Connector for Microsoft Intune to issue PKCS certificates from DigiCert PKI Platform to Intune-managed devices.

item.title: Monitor issued PKI certificates with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Monitor reports for certificates issued via Microsoft Intune cloud PKI.

item.title: Device Action: Rotate local admin password - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to rotate the local admin password on Windows and macOS devices with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Delete issued PKI certificates with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Delete certificates issued via Microsoft Intune cloud PKI.

item.title: View ChromeOS device information - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View ChromeOS devices and details synced with the Chrome Enterprise connector in the Microsoft Intune admin center.

item.title: Overview of Certificate Connector for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the unified Certificate Connector for Microsoft Intune, which supports SCEP, PKCS, imported PKCS, and certificate revocation.

item.title: Get Support for Endpoint Analytics - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get support for Microsoft Intune endpoint analytics, including technical troubleshooting, online help, and product feedback options.

item.title: What's new in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find out what's new in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview of Microsoft Intune device compliance, including tenant-wide compliance policy settings and device compliance policies.

item.title: Monitor results of your device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the device compliance dashboard to understand overall device compliance the per policy and per setting device compliance results.

item.title: Linux device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Linux devices in Intune using the Intune app. Set an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Create device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create device compliance policies for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use custom compliance settings for Linux and Windows devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manage custom compliance settings for Linux and Windows devices by using JSON files and discovery scripts in Intune.

item.title: Deployment guide for Linux device management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use our platform deployment guide to set up Linux device management in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Create a JSON file for custom compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create the JSON file that defines custom settings and values for use with device compliance policies in Intune.

item.title: Linux device compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the device compliance settings for Linux that you can manage with Microsoft Intune compliance policies.

item.title: Supported Microsoft Intune Apps - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article provides lists of support partner and Microsoft apps that are commonly used with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure Microsoft Intune for increased security - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to improve your security posture with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Monitor Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to monitor device compliance and onboarding status for Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Microsoft Intune in the admin center.

item.title: Configure Windows Driver Update Policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to create, approve, deploy, and pause Windows driver updates using Intune policies to keep Windows devices current and stable.

item.title: Step 2. Create App Protection Policies for Microsoft Edge for Business - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 2. Create app protection policies for Microsoft Edge for Business across Windows, Android, and iOS platforms.

item.title: Add Microsoft 365 Apps to Windows Devices Using Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how you can use Microsoft Intune to install Microsoft 365 apps on Windows devices.

item.title: Intune Data Platform Schema - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review the Microsoft Intune data platform schema for device queries and inventory. Discover supported properties and data types to efficiently manage and report on your devices with Intune.

item.title: Configure Platform SSO for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to configure Platform SSO and deploy the configuration to your macOS devices. Platform SSO enables single sign-on (SSO) using Microsoft Entra ID with the Secure Enclave, smart card, or password authentication methods. You create a settings catalog policy to configure the settings. This article is a step-by-step guide to configure Platform SSO for macOS devices using Intune.

item.title: Connect to the Data Warehouse With Power BI - Microsoft Intune
item.description: You can download a file for use with Microsoft Power BI that allows you to load interactive, dynamically generated reports for your Microsoft Intune tenant.

item.title: Use the Intune Data Warehouse - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Intune Data Warehouse to build reports that provide insight into your enterprise mobile environment.

item.title: Create an Intune Report From the OData Feed With Power BI - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create a treemap visualization using Power BI Desktop with an interactive filter from the Intune Data Warehouse API.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for iOS/iPadOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned iOS/iPadOS devices into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Step 5 – Enroll devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Android, Android Enterprise, iOS, iPadOS, Linux, macOS, and Windows devices in Intune.

item.title: Get started with macOS endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune guide to set up and configure macOS devices from setup to creating policies and enrolling devices. You secure your corporate and organization-owned endpoints that are enrolled in Intune, and then deploy at scale with Apple Business Manager or Apple School Manager.

item.title: Device restriction settings for Android in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: On Android Enterprise or Android for Work devices owned by your organization, you can restrict settings on the device using Microsoft Intune. Allow copy and paste, notifications, app permissions, data sharing, password length, sign in failures, use fingerprint to unlock, reuse passwords, and enable bluetooth sharing of work contacts. Configure devices as a dedicated device kiosk to run one app, or multiple apps.

item.title: Device Actions - Wipe, Lock, Locate, and More - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Discover how to use Microsoft Intune to remotely manage, wipe, lock, restart, and secure Android, iOS/iPadOS, macOS, Windows, and ChromeOS devices. Learn about available device actions, prerequisites, and bulk actions for IT admins.

item.title: Device Action: Fresh Start - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use Fresh Start to remove or uninstall apps with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure the Microsoft Managed Home Screen App - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure the Microsoft Managed Home Screen app.

item.title: Device Action: Remove Apps and Configuration - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how apps and configurations can be removed temporarily, then restored automatically or manually using the Remove apps and configuration device action with Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Rename Device - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the Rename device action in Microsoft Intune to update the device name shown in the admin center. Useful for standardizing naming conventions, managing shared devices, and improving inventory clarity.

item.title: Device Action: Remote Lock - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the remote lock action in Microsoft Intune to lock a managed device that has a passcode or PIN.

item.title: Device Action: Reset Passcode - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to reset a passcode with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Send Custom Notification - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune to create and send custom notifications to users of iOS/iPadOS and Android devices.

item.title: Device Action: Sync - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the device sync action in Intune to apply policy, app, and configuration updates to managed devices.

item.title: Creating elevation rules with Endpoint Privilege Management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View guidance on how to create strong file elevation rules with Microsoft Intune Endpoint Privilege Management

item.title: Device Action: Lost Mode - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enable Lost Mode in Microsoft Intune to remotely lock a lost or stolen iOS or iPadOS device and display a custom message and phone number on the lock screen.

item.title: Device Action: Autopilot Reset - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use Autopilot reset with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: BitLocker Key Rotation - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to rotate the BitLocker recovery key with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Restart - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to restart managed devices with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Rotate Recovery Lock Passcode - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to rotate the macOS Recovery Lock passcode with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Remove Passcode - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune remove passcode action helps you unlock devices remotely when users forget their passcodes. Discover how to use this feature.

item.title: Device Action: Run Remediation - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to initiate on demand remediations with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up an iOS/iPadOS ADE token in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create, renew, and delete Apple enrollment program tokens in Microsoft Intune for automated device enrollment on iOS/iPadOS.

item.title: Set up a macOS ADE token in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create, renew, and delete Apple enrollment program tokens for macOS automated device enrollment in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Overview of Apple Automated Device Enrollment for iOS/iPadOS in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about automated device enrollment (ADE) for Apple mobile devices in Microsoft Intune, including supported scenarios, key features, and how to get started.

item.title: Device Action: Full Scan - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to initiate on demand Microsoft Defender full scan with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Logout Current User - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the logout the current user from a Shared iPad with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: New Remote Assistance Session - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the new remote assistance session action in Intune to offer support to your users.

item.title: Device Action: Pause Config Refresh - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to temporarily pause policy enforcement on Windows 11 devices using Intune's Pause Config Refresh action to support troubleshooting and manual changes.

item.title: Manage iOS/iPadOS ADE devices and tokens in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Sync devices, distribute devices, and re-enroll devices for Apple automated device enrollment on iOS/iPadOS in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Manage macOS ADE devices and tokens in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Sync devices, assign enrollment profiles, and distribute Mac computers for Apple automated device enrollment in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Remove User - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to remove a user from a Shared iPad with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Play Lost Mode Sound - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the Play lost device sound action in Microsoft Intune to trigger an audible alert on a lost, stolen, or misplaced device—helping users locate it quickly and securely.

item.title: Device Action: Quick Scan - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to initiate on demand Microsoft Defender quick scan with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: macOS device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll macOS devices using device enrollment, automated device enrollment (DEP), and Apple Configurator enrollment options in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Device Action: Disable Activation Lock - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use Microsoft Intune to disable Activation Lock on Apple devices.

item.title: Overview of Apple Automated Device Enrollment for macOS in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about automated device enrollment (ADE) for Mac computers in Microsoft Intune, including supported scenarios, key features, and how to get started.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll iOS and iPadOS devices using user and device enrollment, automated device enrollment (DEP), and Apple Configurator in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for macOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned Macs into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Device Action: Rotate FileVault Recovery Key - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to rotate the FileVault recovery key with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Action: Deprovision - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to deprovision a chromeOS device with Microsoft Intune.
